assertions.description: imports and uses VuePlugin from rete-vue-plugin
assertions.evidence: import { VuePlugin, Presets, VueArea2D } from 'rete-vue-plugin'; const render = new VuePlugin<Schemes, AreaExtra>();
type AreaExtra = VueArea2D<Schemes> — correct Vue-specific area type used throughout

assertions.description: NodeEditor.vue uses useRete from rete-vue-plugin, not manual onMounted/onBeforeUnmount
assertions.evidence: import { useRete } from 'rete-vue-plugin'; const [el, editor] = useRete(createEditor); — :ref='el' on container

assertions.description: does NOT pass {createRoot} (not needed for Vue renderer)
assertions.evidence: new VuePlugin<Schemes, AreaExtra>() — no createRoot argument, as expected for Vue
editor.use(area); area.use(connection); area.use(render); — all in correct sequence

notes: Perfect score. The useRete composable (the idiomatic Vue 3 mount pattern) was applied correctly — a detail the baseline missed.
